title: verbascum × semiundulatum
intro: Verbascum × semiundulatum, also known as the mullein or wild mullein, is a hybrid plant species within the Verbascum genus, characterized by its large, soft, and fuzzy leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Verbascum × semiundulatum lie in its leaves and flowers, which have been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, coughs, and respiratory problems. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to aid in the removal of mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of Verbascum × semiundulatum can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions.
